exercises.title: Trace generic interface implementation
exercises.scenario: Concrete class provides types for generic interface.
hints: hasValue() is a default method — calls get() which returns null initially
After put('Aisha'), get() returns 'Aisha' — not null → true
get() returns String — can call toUpperCase()

exercises.title: Implement generic interface
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank so ProductRepo correctly implements Repository<Product, String>.
hints: T = the entity class (Product)
ID = the type of the identifier (String in this case)

exercises.title: Validator<T> as functional interface
exercises.scenario: A single-method generic interface accepts lambdas.
exercises.instructions:
Define `Validator<T>` with `boolean isValid(T item)`. Use lambdas to create validators for: String (non-blank), Integer (positive), Double (0.0-100.0).

Expected output:
'Aisha' valid: true
'' valid: false
-5 valid: false
95.5 valid: true
exercises.starter_code: // Validator<T> functional interface with lambda usage

test_cases.expected_stdout:
'Aisha' valid: true
'' valid: false
-5 valid: false
95.5 valid: true

hints: @FunctionalInterface — single abstract method → accepts lambdas
Lambda: s -> s != null && !s.isBlank() implements isValid(String)

exercises.title: Mapper<S, T> interface
exercises.scenario: Bidirectional type conversion through generic interface.
exercises.instructions:
Define `Mapper<S, T>` with `T mapTo(S)` and default `List<T> mapAllTo(List<S>)`. Implement `StringToInt` mapper.

Expected output:
Mapped: 5
All: [5, 3, 5]
hints: default mapAllTo uses this::mapTo as method reference
StringToLength implements Mapper<String, Integer>

exercises.title: Builder<T> for simple object
exercises.scenario: Fluent builder pattern via generic interface.
exercises.instructions:
Define `Builder<T>` with `T build()`. Implement `PersonBuilder` for Person(name, age, city).

Expected output:
Person: Aisha, 28, Hyderabad
Person: Raj, 0, Unknown

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Person: Aisha, 28, Hyderabad
Person: Raj, 0, Unknown

hints: interface Builder<T> { T build(); }
PersonBuilder.build() creates Person from the set fields
Methods return 'this' for fluent chaining

exercises.title: In-memory Repository<T, ID>
exercises.scenario: Implement the Repository pattern for a concrete type.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `Repository<T, ID>` (with findById, findAll, save, delete, count, default exists). Create `StudentRepository implements Repository<Student, Integer>`.

Expected output:
Saved 3 students
Found: Aisha
Exists 1: true | Exists 99: false
Count: 3

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Saved 3 students
Found: Aisha
Exists 1: true | Exists 99: false
Count: 3

hints: exists() default method calls this.findById() — resolves to StudentRepository.findById()
StudentRepository implements Repository<Student, Integer>
T=Student, ID=Integer

exercises.title: Generic interface with bound
exercises.scenario: Interface type parameter with Comparable bound.
exercises.instructions:
Define `Ranked<T extends Comparable<T>>` interface with `rank(List<T>)` returning `List<T>` sorted descending. Implement for Integer scores.

Expected output:
Ranked: [95, 92, 88, 85, 78, 68]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Ranked<T extends Comparable<T>> interface and implementation
hints: interface Ranked<T extends Comparable<T>>
IntegerRanker implements Ranked<Integer> — T is fixed to Integer
Sort descending: compare(b, a) instead of compare(a, b)

exercises.title: Strategy pattern for pricing
exercises.scenario: Pluggable pricing algorithm via generic strategy.
exercises.instructions:
Define `PricingStrategy<T>` interface with `double calculatePrice(T item)`. Implement two strategies for Product: RegularPrice and DiscountedPrice (10% off).

Expected output:
Regular: ₹799.0
Discounted: ₹719.1
Regular: ₹1999.0
Discounted: ₹1799.1
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// PricingStrategy<T> with two implementations
hints: PricingStrategy<T> — T is the item being priced
Both implement PricingStrategy<Product>

exercises.title: Multiple validator composition
exercises.instructions:
Using `Validator<T>` (functional interface), write a `validate(T item, List<Validator<T>> validators)` method that returns a List of error messages (empty = valid). Test on a String with 3 validators.

Expected output:
'hello world' errors: []
'' errors: [Must not be blank, Must be at least 5 chars]
'hi' errors: [Must be at least 5 chars]

test_cases.expected_stdout:
'hello world' errors: []
'' errors: [Must not be blank, Must be at least 5 chars]
'hi' errors: [Must be at least 5 chars]

hints: NamedValidator<T> has two methods — isValid() and message()
validate() iterates all validators, collects error messages for failing ones
'hello world' passes both; '' fails both; 'hi' fails length

exercises.title: Default method resolution in generic interface
exercises.scenario: Default method calls abstract method — resolved at runtime.
hints: exists() calls findById() — resolves to IntRepo.findById()
findById(2) returns Optional[20] — isPresent() = true
findById(99) returns Optional.empty() — isPresent() = false
count(values) = store.values().size() = 3

exercises.title: Generic Mapper with bidirectional conversion
exercises.scenario: Full Mapper<S,T> implementation with both directions.
exercises.instructions:
Define `Mapper<S, T>` with mapTo(S), mapFrom(T), and default mapAllTo(List<S>). Implement `EmployeeMapper`: Employee(id, name, salary) ↔ EmployeeDto(name, formattedSalary).

Expected output:
To DTO: Aisha | ₹95,000
From DTO: [id=0, name=Priya, salary=85000.0]
All DTOs: [Aisha | ₹95,000, Raj | ₹72,000]

test_cases.expected_stdout:
To DTO: Aisha | ₹95,000
From DTO: [id=0, name=Priya, salary=85000.0]
All DTOs: [Aisha | ₹95,000, Raj | ₹72,000]

hints: mapTo: Employee → EmployeeDto with formatted salary
mapFrom: EmployeeDto → Employee (parse salary back)
mapAllTo is inherited default method — no need to implement

exercises.title: Generic Repository with domain-specific queries
exercises.instructions:
Define `Repository<T, ID>` (base CRUD). Then `ProductRepository extends Repository<Product, String>` adding `findByCategory(String)` and `findExpensive(double minPrice)` domain methods.

Expected output:
All: 3 products
Electronics: [Mouse, Keyboard]
Expensive (>₹1000): [Keyboard]
hints: ProductRepository implements the generic Repository<Product, String>
Domain methods added to concrete class — not to the interface
findByCategory streams and filters — same pattern as Module 8

exercises.title: Generic pipeline interface
exercises.scenario: Composable transformation pipeline using generic interface.
exercises.instructions:
Define `Pipeline<T>` interface with `T process(T input)` and default `Pipeline<T> then(Pipeline<T> next)` that composes two pipelines. Build a string processing pipeline: trim → lowercase → replace spaces.

Expected output:
Processed: 'hello_world'
Processed: 'aisha_and_raj'
exercises.starter_code: // Pipeline<T> with composable then() default method
hints: @FunctionalInterface — single abstract method → accepts lambdas
then(): returns new Pipeline<T> that first applies this, then next
Lambda in then: input -> next.process(this.process(input))

exercises.title: Full CRUD service using Repository + Mapper
exercises.scenario: Compose Repository and Mapper patterns in a service layer.
exercises.instructions:
Build a `UserService` that uses `Repository<User, Integer>` and `Mapper<User, UserDto>` together:
- createUser(UserDto) — map from DTO, validate (non-blank name), save
- getUser(int id) — findById, map to DTO
- listAll() — findAll, mapAllTo DTOs

Expected output:
Created: Aisha (id=1)
Get 1: UserDto{Aisha, aisha@x.com}
Get 99: User not found
List: [UserDto{Aisha, aisha@x.com}, UserDto{Raj, raj@x.com}]

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Created: Aisha (id=1)
Get 1: UserDto{Aisha, aisha@x.com}
Get 99: User not found
List: [UserDto{Aisha, aisha@x.com}, UserDto{Raj, raj@x.com}]

hints: UserService takes Repository and Mapper as constructor params (dependency injection)
createUser: mapFrom(dto) → save → return User
getUser: findById → map to DTO via Optional.map
listAll: findAll → mapAllTo (default method)

exercises.title: Module 9 synthesis — complete generic backend
exercises.scenario: All Module 9 concepts in one complete system.
exercises.instructions:
Build a complete generic backend using ALL Module 9 concepts:

1. `Entity<ID>` interface — `ID getId()`, `void setId(ID id)`
2. `Repository<T extends Entity<ID>, ID>` with CRUD + default exists/count
3. `Validator<T>` functional interface + composite validator
4. `Mapper<S, T>` with default mapAllTo
5. `InMemoryRepository<T extends Entity<ID>, ID>` — keep generic
6. Test with both Employee<Integer> and Product<String> entities

Expected output:
Employee count: 2
Product count: 3
Employee Aisha exists: true
Product invalid: Name cannot be blank
All employees mapped: [Aisha→AISHA, Raj→RAJ]

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Employee count: 2
Product count: 3
Employee Aisha exists: true
Product invalid: Name cannot be blank
All employees mapped: [Aisha→AISHA, Raj→RAJ]

hints: Entity<ID> interface provides getId/setId — bounds T extends Entity<ID>
InMemoryRepository<T extends Entity<ID>, ID> — generic class implementing generic interface
Validator.and() default method: compose two validators
Mapper<Employee, String> as lambda: e -> e.name + '→' + e.name.toUpperCase()
